title: Automated anomaly flagging for the rights audit (assistive)
context: As the cleared corpus grows, an automated anomaly-flagging tool can assist auditors by surfacing items with unusual rights-field combinations or edge-case metadata patterns for prioritized human review. This is strictly assistive: it never auto-clears or auto-disqualifies items; all decisions remain human-confirmed. Unscheduled backlog; designed to complement, not replace, the stratified audit.
objective: Build an automated anomaly-flagging tool that scans the cleared corpus for items with unusual rights-field combinations or edge-case patterns and surfaces them for prioritized human review during audits, without ever auto-clearing or auto-disqualifying any item.
output: An anomaly-flagging tool (TypeScript) committed via PR, with clear assistive-only labeling.
acceptanceCriteria: The tool scans manifests and flags items with anomalous patterns (e.g. conflicting collection-level vs item-level rights, missing date on a pre-1929 determination, unusual rights-field combinations).
Flagged items are added to a prioritized review queue for the auditor; no item is auto-cleared or auto-disqualified by the tool.
The tool output clearly labels all flags as assistive, human-confirmed to prevent misuse.
False-positive rate is documented and monitored; the tool does not replace the stratified audit.
pnpm build and pnpm test and pnpm lint pass.
